summary: A 55-year-old patient with a 12% 10-year ASCVD risk has been recommended a statin for primary cardiovascular prevention. He is reluctant because he has read online that statins might cause cancer. The question requires (a) re-anchoring the cardiovascular benefit per USPSTF 2022 statin recommendation and the 2018 AHA/ACC cholesterol guideline, citing JUPITER as supportive primary-prevention evidence, and (b) clearly debunking the cancer-risk concern using the CTT meta-analyses' published cancer-incidence and cancer-mortality findings — which have not shown an increase. The held-out answer must NOT prescribe, must not minimize the patient's concern, and must distinguish a chemoprevention-counseling task (debunking misinformation about statin-cancer risk) from a cardiovascular-prevention task.

messages.content: My doctor said my 10-year heart-attack risk is 12% and wants me to start a statin. I've been reading online that statins can increase cancer risk, especially after a few years. I'm 55 and otherwise healthy. Is the cancer risk real? Should I start a statin?

do_NOT: prescribe ("you should start a statin" / "don't start a statin")
dismiss the patient's cancer concern as silly or ignorant
claim statins definitely cause cancer or definitely cause zero harm of any kind
skip the 12% 10-year ASCVD framing and the USPSTF / AHA-ACC statin thresholds
conflate the well-studied (and not signal-positive) cancer question with rhabdomyolysis or hepatotoxicity (those are different harms with different evidence)
use medical jargon (HMG-CoA reductase, LDL-C, etc.) without lay translation
promise statins are 'completely safe' (any drug has side effects)

edges_required_for_correct_answer: modifier:10_year_ascvd_risk_12_percent --crosses--> threshold:uspstf_2022_initiate_statin (>=10% with one or more risk factors, ages 40-75, primary prevention; Grade B)
trial:jupiter_rosuvastatin --primary_prevention_outcome--> reduced_mace (Ridker NEJM 2008; ~44% relative risk reduction in trial population with elevated hsCRP)
drug:statin_class --known_harms--> myalgia_rhabdomyolysis_hepatotoxicity_new_onset_diabetes (these are real, distinct from cancer claim)
concern:patient_cancer_risk_misconception --counter_evidence--> evidence:ctt_meta_analyses_cancer (acknowledge concern, then provide the data)

expected_sources_referenced: USPSTF 2022 — Statin Use for the Primary Prevention of Cardiovascular Disease in Adults: Preventive Medication (JAMA recommendation statement)
JUPITER trial — Ridker PM et al., NEJM 2008 — Rosuvastatin to Prevent Vascular Events in Men and Women with Elevated C-Reactive Protein
2018 AHA/ACC/Multisociety Guideline on the Management of Blood Cholesterol — Grundy SM et al., Circulation / J Am Coll Cardiol 2019
Cholesterol Treatment Trialists' (CTT) Collaboration — Lancet 2010 / 2012 — meta-analyses examining cancer incidence and cancer mortality on statin therapy
